cue: When the sum is under target, every partner between the two cursors also qualifies; add them all at once.
Two pointers on the sorted array; a small sum counts a whole block, a large sum shrinks the high end.
prompt: Write `countPairsLess(a, target)` where `a` is sorted ascending. Return the number of index pairs `(i, j)` with `i < j` whose values sum to strictly less than `target`. Use two pointers; do not use a nested loop.

cue: Two pointers from the ends, but slide each past any non-alphanumeric character before comparing.
prompt: Write `isPalAlnum(s)` that returns `true` if `s` reads the same forward and backward when you ignore every non-alphanumeric character and treat upper and lower case as equal. Use two pointers from the ends.

cue: Two pointers from the ends; advance a cursor when it is not on a vowel, and swap only when both sit on vowels.
prompt: Write `reverseVowels(s)` that returns `s` with only its vowels reversed and every other character left in place. Vowels are `aeiou` in either case. Use two pointers from the ends.

cue: Opposite ends: leave a correctly placed end alone, and swap only when both ends are misplaced.
Skip a low end that is already negative and a high end that is already non-negative; swap the pair that is out of place.
prompt: Write `partitionSign(a)` that rearranges `a` in place so every negative value comes before every non-negative value, mutating `a` and returning it. Use two pointers from the ends, swapping a misplaced pair. Order within each group need not be preserved.

concept: keep at most two of each (sorted)
cue: Read/write compaction, but compare against the value two slots back so each value can survive twice.
prompt: Write `removeDupesTwo(a)` where `a` is sorted ascending. Overwrite `a` in place so each value appears at most twice, keeping order, and return the new length `k`. Use a read pointer and a lagging write pointer.

cue: Fix one element, converge two pointers on the rest, and keep the running sum whose distance to target is smallest.
